content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS with length NNN. You should find two non-intersecting substrings of SSS such that the second one is a substring of the first one and the product of these substrings' lengths is the maximum possible. More formally, let's denote a contiguous substring Sl,Sl+1,…,SrS_l, S_{l+1}, \ldots, S_rSl​,Sl+1​,…,Sr​ by S[l,r]S[l, r]S[l,r]; you should find integers l1l_1l1​, r1r_1r1​, l2l_2l2​ and r2r_2r2​ which satisfy these four criteria:


1≤l1≤r1≤N1 \le l_1 \le r_1 \le N1≤l1​≤r1​≤N and 1≤l2≤r2≤N1 \le l_2 \le r_2 \le N1≤l2​≤r2​≤N
r2<l1r_2 \lt l_1r2​<l1​ or r1<l2r_1 \lt l_2r1​<l2​
S[l2,r2]S[l_2, r_2]S[l2​,r2​] is a contiguous substring of S[l1,r1]S[l_1, r_1]S[l1​,r1​]
the product P=(r2−l2+1)⋅(r1−l1+1)P = (r_2-l_2+1) \cdot (r_1-l_1+1)P=(r2​−l2​+1)⋅(r1​−l1​+1) is maximum possible
Find the maximum value of PPP. It is guaranteed that for the given test data, there is at least one way to choose l1,r1,l2,r2l_1, r_1, l_2, r_2l1​,r1​,l2​,r2​ such that the first three criteria are satisfied, i.e. the answer is well-defined.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
`ExplanationWe can choose substrings S[1,2]S[1, 2]S[1,2] and S[3,5]S[3, 5]S[3,5].
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum product PPP.
